title: Create a Snippet
text: Create a Snippet You can create and organize snippets of ProcScript, JavaScript, HTML other text in snippet libraries. You must have created a project to contain your application definitions. For more information, see Working with Projects . Open your project. In the U-Bar, click Browse , select prj , then choose the project you want to open. If you have not already done so, create or open an IncludeScript Library: In the Templates tab of the Resource Browser, select the Snippet library template. Drag and drop it onto the project in the Structure Editor. Rename the library. Optionally, edit the Description property to provide a more informative description. Open the Snippet Library ( libsnp ). Right-click it and choose Open from the menu. Drag and drop a Snippet template from the Resource Browser onto the Library object in the Structure pane. By default, the Resource Browser shows the templates of the UPALETTE_LIBSNP palette. Rename the snippet using the format Name.Syntax , where Syntax indicates the syntax of the content. For example, mylayout.html Any syntax extension can be used, such as html , procscript , js , txt , and so on. However, syntax highlighting is available only for snippets with the html or procscript extension. Note: There is no restriction on the syntax of snippets that a library can contain. The libraries provided by Uniface contain snippets of the same type, but you can choose to organize snippets in whatever way you find useful. In the Properties Inspector, edit the Description property to provide more information about the snippet. In the Write Script worksheet, select the newly created snippet in the Structure pane, and edit its contents. Notice that you can use snippets to edit your new snippet. The snippets you create in a snippet library can only be made available for use in the Uniface IDE if the snippet library is defined as a palette. For more information, see Define Palettes and Templates . Related Links Snippets Snippets are named fragments of code or text that you can insert into your application script or layout to speed development. They are defined as development objects in snippet libraries. Naming Rules for Uniface Development Objects The name of a development object is its unique identifier. When naming development objects, use the allowed syntax for the object name. Avoid using words that are reserved for use within Uniface and your own environment, including the operating system and database. Libraries Libraries are collections of objects that can be shared in some way, either during development, or at runtime. They are main development objects, so whenever they are saved or exported, all the objects that they contain are included. Add Objects from the Resource Browser You can select templates, modeled objects, and code snippets in the Resource Browser and add them to current object. You can drag and drop objects, or use the context menu. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
